heading: Filtering Considerations
body:
- MongoDB Vector Search supports the short form of [$eq](manual/reference/operator/query/eq). In the short
  form, you don't need to specify `$eq` in the query.

  For example, consider the following filter with `$eq`:

  ```json
  "filter": { "_id": { "$eq": ObjectId("5a9427648b0beebeb69537a5") }
  ```

  This is equivalent to the following filter, which uses the short
  form of `$eq`:

  ```json
  "filter": { "_id": ObjectId("5a9427648b0beebeb69537a5") }
  ```

- You can use the [$and](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/and) MQL (MongoDB Query Language) operator to specify an
  array of filters in a single query.

  For example, consider the following pre-filter for documents with a
  `genres` field equal to `Action` and a `year` field with the
  value `1999`, `2000`, or `2001`:

  ```json
  "filter": {
    "$and": [
      { "genres": "Action" },
      { "year": { "$in": [ 1999, 2000, 2001 ] } }
    ]
  }
  ```

- For advanced filtering capabilities such as fuzzy search, phrase
  matching, location filtering, and other analyzed text, use the
  [vectorSearch](fts-vectorSearch-ref) operator in a
  [$search](pipe.$search) stage.

We recommend dedicated [Search Nodes](configure-search-nodes)
to isolate vector search query processing. You might see improved query
performance on the dedicated Search Nodes. Note that the high-CPU systems
might provide more performance improvement. When MongoDB Vector Search runs on search
nodes, MongoDB Vector Search parallelizes query execution across segments of data.

Parallelization of query processing improves the response time in many
cases, such as queries on large datasets. Using intra-query parallelism
during MongoDB Vector Search query processing utilizes more resources, but improves
latency for each individual query.

> **Note:** MongoDB Vector Search doesn't guarantee that each query will run concurrently. For
> example, when too many concurrent queries are queued, MongoDB Vector Search might
> fall back to single-threaded execution.
>
> You might see inconsistent results for the same successive queries.
> To mitigate this, increase the value of `numCandidates` in your
> [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) queries.
